books.title: Poetry
books.author: Various
books.rationale: Lesson plans draw in additional poetry from the 19th century to broaden the student's encounter with poetry from this time period.

books.title: The Lightning Dreamer: Cuba's Greatest Abolitionist
books.author: Margarita Engle
books.rationale: This book is a poetic fictionalized account of famous Cuban abolitionist and author Gertrudis Gómez Avellaneda (1814-1873). Since Avellaneda wrote in Spanish, this biography allows students to get a glimpse of a significant 19th century author outside the English canon. Engle's style also provides opportunities for students to consider the differences between poetry and prose and experiment with point of view in connected composition assignments.

books.title: The Crescent Moon
books.author: Rabindranath Tagore
books.rationale: This delightful collection of children's poetry was translated from Bengali into English by Tagore himself. Lesson plans will draw from this collection and include links to additional poetry by this famous 19th century author.

books.title: The Aeneid
books.author: Virgil
books.rationale: The famous epic poem in which Virgil seeks to outdo Homer and build the glory of Rome. This text complements this year's studies in ancient history which cover the Roman period.

books.title: Jump Back, Paul: The Life and Poems of Paul Laurence Dunbar
books.author: Sally Derby
books.rationale: An engaging narrative telling about the life and times of Paul Laurence Dunbar, one of the most widely acclaimed American poets at the end of the 19th century. Dunbar's poetry is woven throughout the narrative so students can understand its context and significance.
